memoryArtifacts.description: Ralph was a successful wool merchant who purchased piecemeal a coherent manorial estate in the south Nottinghamshire village of Willoughby on the Wold.

memoryArtifacts.headline: History
memoryArtifacts.description:
The Bingham Family of Nottingham

zh.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: 英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Willoughby III於大約1206年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲時，他的父親Richard de Willoughby II27歲，母親Agnes de Wollaton24歲。他與Juliana of Nottinghamshire結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1248年在英國諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Willoughby III于大约1206年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲时，他的父亲Richard de Willoughby II27岁，母亲Agnes de Wollaton24岁。他与Juliana of Nottinghamshire结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1248年在英国諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Willoughby III was born about 1206, in Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Richard de Willoughby II, was 27 and his mother, Agnes de Wollaton, was 24. He married Juliana of Nottinghamshire. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1248, in Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 43.

Il a épousé Juliana of Nottinghamshire. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1248, à Bingham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Willoughby III(이)가 대략 1206년 영국 노틴그함실르 윌루그h비  테 울드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Willoughby II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Wollaton(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Juliana of Nottinghamshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1248년 영국 노틴그함실르 빙함에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.
